hook.text: Google launched Google+ in 2011 to challenge Facebook. It reached 90 million sign-ups and almost zero engagement.

buildup.text: Google+ had 'Circles' for organizing friends into groups — a genuine improvement over Facebook's flat friend list.

discovery.text: But social networks live on network effects. Your friends were already on Facebook. An empty social network has no value.

twist.text: Google forced Plus into YouTube, Gmail, and Android. Users resented being pushed into a social network they didn't choose.

climax.text: Google+ became a ghost town with inflated metrics. Google shut it down in 2019 after a data breach provided a convenient exit.

punchline.text: You can't force a social network. The people have to come willingly.

quiz.question: What was Google+'s core product failure?
options: Users' friends were already on Facebook — an empty social network has no value
Google+ had worse features than Facebook
The technology behind Google+ was unreliable
